raw.objective: To describe LVT pharmacokinetics in neonates
raw.covariates: Included: WT on CL and Vd, AGE on CL
raw.observations: Due to the mathematical description of clearance increase due to age in days, it is unsure if the clearance keeps rising in that manner after 5 days after being born. The model seems to work well for their specific population, but may not predict as well for older neonates, let alone infants and older patients. The statistical and covariate model are not fully described, which makes our description of the model in the next table only an approximation.
raw.structure: 1 compartment with linear elimination and no absorption (IV)
raw.equations: Vd/F=theta1*WT CL/F=theta2*WT*((AGE/5)**theta3)* (1+eta1) Cobs=Cipred *The exact covariate model was not described, we have aimed to approximate as best as possible using the available information in the paper. Furthermore, no information on the statistical model was given, other than that eta was only estimated for CL AGE is in days after being born

extraction_target.title: A seven-day study of the pharmacokinetics of intravenous levetiracetam in neonates: marked changes in pharmacokinetics occur during the first week of life.

structural_model.description: 1 compartment with linear elimination and no absorption (IV)

omega.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.objective: To describe LVT pharmacokinetics in neonates

covariates.raw: Included: WT on CL and Vd, AGE on CL

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
